title: Smoky British Lentil Frittata
description: A hearty, smoky frittata brimming with lentils, poppy seeds, and a hint of bhuna spice—perfect for a cozy brunch.
instructions: Soak lentils in warm water for 30 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat; add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and sauté for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in soaked lentils and 1 tsp bhuna spice; cook for 2-3 minutes, then add 200ml water, bring to a simmer, and cook until lentils are tender, about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, whisk eggs, milk, poppy seeds, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
When lentils are cooked, pour the egg mixture over them in the saucepan, stirring gently to combine.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let the mixture set for 4-5 minutes until the edges start to firm up.
Sprinkle grated cheddar cheese on top, then transfer the pan to a preheated oven at 180°C (350°F) and bake for 8-10 minutes until the frittata is set and lightly golden.
Remove from oven, let it cool for a minute, then garnish with fresh thyme or parsley.
Slice and serve warm, optionally with a side of crusty bread or a simple green salad.
